description:
Expose the typer's per-spec reachability (`FnTypes.reachable_blocks` at src/ir_typer.rs:1741-1772) at module level so other passes don't have to re-derive it.

## Why

Today reachability lives inside per-spec FnTypes; the dump and pattern_check both need it but can't see it cleanly. main.rs `compute_survivors` (line 80-92) is a third independent derivation of the same idea.

## Shape

Add to Module (or ModuleTypes):

```rust
reachable_specs: HashSet<SpecKey>,
reachable_blocks_per_spec: HashMap<SpecKey, HashSet<BlockId>>,
```

Populated by the typer at the same point it currently computes `reachable_blocks` internally. No new analysis — just promote what's already computed to a public structure.

## Verify

- No behavior change.
- Add a unit test pinning reachable_specs for a small fixture (e.g. multi_clause: classify+fact bodies should not be in reachable_specs if main fully folds — wait, they ARE reachable as user fns; reachability is about call graph, not "did the reducer consume me." Clarify in the ticket comment if needed.)
- Actually two reachability notions exist: (a) the typer's branch-deadness inside a spec; (b) post-reduction "is there any caller left for this fn." Decide whether the field captures (a), (b), or both, and name accordingly.

## Independent

No dependencies. Blocks .7 and .8.
title: fz-RS.6 — Lift typer reachability to module level
